description:
You are a data-engineering assistant.  
  A stub ETL workspace is already present on disk under /home/user/etl/, containing a raw JSON data set and a JSON-Schema definition.  
  Your goal is to 1) validate the raw data against the schema, 2) separate valid from invalid records with jq, 3) calculate simple metrics, and 4) write a small, machine-checkable log so that an automated grader can confirm the result.

  File/Directory layout that already exists when you start:
    /home/user/etl/
        ├── raw/
        │     └── events.json              (array-style JSON; 5 objects)
        └── schema/
              └── event_schema.json        (draft-07 JSON Schema)

  Target artefacts you must create:
    1. Directory  : /home/user/etl/processed/
    2. Directory  : /home/user/etl/logs/
    3. File       : /home/user/etl/processed/events_valid.jsonl
                   • NEW-LINE-DELIMITED JSON (a.k.a. NDJSON).  
                   • Each **valid** event object must occupy exactly one line.  
                   • Preserve every field as it appears in the original object—no extra or reordered keys.
    4. File       : /home/user/etl/processed/summary_metrics.json  
                   • A single JSON object with three integer keys, exactly in the following order:  
                       {  
                         "total_events": <int>,  
                         "valid_events": <int>,  
                         "invalid_events": <int>  
                       }
    5. File       : /home/user/etl/logs/validation.log  
                   • Three plain-text lines, strictly:  
                       TOTAL: <int>  
                       VALID: <int>  
                       INVALID: <int>  

  Business rules for validity (encode these either via jq or by invoking a validator of your choice):
    • id        – string matching the regexp  ^[a-f0-9-]{36}$  
    • timestamp – string that is a valid ISO-8601 date-time (e.g. 2023-06-01T12:00:00Z)  
    • type      – one of "click", "view", "purchase"  
    • payload   – must be an object (content not validated further)  

  Step-by-step overview (you decide on the exact commands):
    a. Inspect /home/user/etl/raw/events.json and /home/user/etl/schema/event_schema.json  
    b. Validate every element in the array using the above rules.  
    c. Emit all passing objects to events_valid.jsonl (NDJSON, one object per line, no extra whitespace).  
    d. Compute and write the metrics file summary_metrics.json using strict key ordering shown above.  
    e. Write validation.log using the exact three-line template.  
    f. Create any missing directories (processed/ and logs/) so that the test harness can find its targets.

  Verification notes:
    • The grader will parse events_valid.jsonl with jq ‑Rsa and ensure exactly 3 lines exist, each line a JSON object identical (byte-for-byte, except for newline) to the corresponding source object.  
    • The grader will cat summary_metrics.json | jq -c and expect the object {"total_events":5,"valid_events":3,"invalid_events":2}.  
    • The grader will run cat validation.log and expect precisely:
          TOTAL: 5
          VALID: 3
          INVALID: 2
      (three lines, upper-case labels, single space after colon).  
    • No other files are examined, so feel free to keep scratch/temporary material elsewhere.

  Important constraints:
    • Work entirely as the normal user (no sudo).  
    • Use only bash-compatible shell commands and standard CLI tools available in a minimal Linux environment (jq is present).  
    • Do NOT modify the original raw files or schema file.  
    • Ensure all paths used are absolute or are relative to /home/user/etl/ for portability.  
    • The grading environment is case-sensitive; match filenames and directory names exactly as stated.

  Begin when ready.  When everything is finished, the grader will simply look for the three target files and their exact content, as described above.
truth:
<!--
    Ground-truth used by the automated grader.
    The agent must create the processed artefacts exactly as below.

    Pre-existing files (already on disk before the agent starts)
  -->
  <preexisting>
    <file path="/home/user/etl/raw/events.json"><![CDATA[
[
  {
    "id": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000",
    "timestamp": "2023-06-01T12:00:00Z",
    "type": "click",
    "payload": {
      "element_id": "btn_signup"
    }
  },
  {
    "id": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174001",
    "timestamp": "2023-06-01T12:05:00Z",
    "type": "view",
    "payload": {
      "page": "/home"
    }
  },
  {
    "id": "not-a-uuid",
    "timestamp": "2023-06-01T12:10:00Z",
    "type": "purchase",
    "payload": {
      "amount": 19.99
    }
  },
  {
    "id": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174002",
    "timestamp": "invalid-date",
    "type": "click",
    "payload": {
      "element_id": "btn_login"
    }
  },
  {
    "id": "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174003",
    "timestamp": "2023-06-01T12:15:00Z",
    "type": "purchase",
    "payload": {
      "amount": 49.99
    }
  }
]
    ]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/etl/schema/event_schema.json"><![CDATA[
{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
  "title": "Event",
  "type": "object",
  "required": ["id", "timestamp", "type", "payload"],
  "properties": {
    "id": {
      "type": "string",
      "pattern": "^[a-f0-9-]{36}$"
    },
    "timestamp": {
      "type": "string",
      "format": "date-time"
    },
    "type": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": ["click", "view", "purchase"]
    },
    "payload": {
      "type": "object"
    }
  },
  "additionalProperties": true
}
    ]]></file>
  </preexisting>

  <!--
    Files/directories the agent must create.
  -->
  <expected>
    <dir>/home/user/etl/processed</dir>
    <dir>/home/user/etl/logs</dir>

    <file path="/home/user/etl/processed/events_valid.jsonl"><![CDATA[
{"id":"123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000","timestamp":"2023-06-01T12:00:00Z","type":"click","payload":{"element_id":"btn_signup"}}
{"id":"123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174001","timestamp":"2023-06-01T12:05:00Z","type":"view","payload":{"page":"/home"}}
{"id":"123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174003","timestamp":"2023-06-01T12:15:00Z","type":"purchase","payload":{"amount":49.99}}
    ]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/etl/processed/summary_metrics.json"><![CDATA[
{
  "total_events": 5,
  "valid_events": 3,
  "invalid_events": 2
}
    ]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/etl/logs/validation.log"><![CDATA[
TOTAL: 5
VALID: 3
INVALID: 2
    ]]></file>
  </expected>
